questions.question: Define Telnet and explain its primary purpose in computer networks.
keyPoints: Telnet stands for TELecommunication NETwork
It provides bidirectional interactive text-oriented communication
Enables users to access and manage remote systems virtually
Acts as a virtual terminal connection between local client and remote server

questions.question: What is Network Virtual Terminal (NVT) in Telnet? Explain its significance.
keyPoints: NVT is a virtual terminal device providing standard interface
It abstracts differences between various terminal types
Converts local terminal input to standard NVT format
Allows interoperability between heterogeneous systems

questions.question: List and explain any three security concerns associated with using Telnet.
keyPoints: Data transmitted in plain text (no encryption)
Passwords visible to network sniffers
Vulnerable to man-in-the-middle attacks
No strong authentication mechanism
Can be intercepted easily

questions.question: Explain Telnet option negotiation commands: WILL, WONT, DO, and DONT.
keyPoints: WILL indicates willingness to enable an option
WONT indicates refusal to enable an option
DO requests the other party to enable an option
DONT refuses or indicates not enabling an option
Used for negotiating optional facilities like echo, terminal type

questions.question: Differentiate between Telnet and SSH protocols.
keyPoints: Telnet uses port 23, SSH uses port 22
Telnet transmits plain text, SSH encrypts all data
SSH provides secure authentication, Telnet does not
SSH has replaced Telnet for secure remote access
Telnet is simpler, SSH is more complex but secure

questions.question: What is the purpose of the escape character in Telnet? How is it used?
keyPoints: Default escape character is Ctrl+]
Used to enter Telnet command mode from session
Allows user to issue Telnet commands without terminating session
Common commands in command mode: quit, close, status, help

questions.question: Describe the process of establishing a Telnet connection.
keyPoints: Client initiates connection to server on port 23
Server accepts connection and sends welcome message
NVT connection is established
Optional terminal type negotiation occurs
Interactive session begins for command exchange

questions.question: Explain in detail the Telnet protocol architecture, including its operation, commands, and security aspects.
keyPoints: Telnet operates at Application Layer (OSI)
Uses NVT concept for standardization
Connection through port 23
Commands: OPEN, CLOSE, QUIT, STATUS, MODE, etc.
Option negotiation with WILL/WONT/DO/DONT
Security: plain text transmission, password vulnerability
Comparison with SSH for secure communication
Mention RFC 854 standard

questions.question: Discuss the Telnet option negotiation mechanism in detail with examples.
keyPoints: Option negotiation allows optional facilities
Three phases: Request, Response, Confirmation
Commands: WILL (enable locally), WONT (refuse local), DO (request remote), DONT (refuse remote)
Common options: Echo, Binary, Terminal Type, Window Size
IAC (Interpret As Command) prefix for special sequences
Example: Client sends DO ECHO, Server responds WILL ECHO
Negotiation ensures mutual agreement before enabling options

questions.question: Explain the working of Telnet with a neat diagram. Also discuss why Telnet is considered insecure and what alternatives exist.
keyPoints: Diagram showing client-server architecture
Connection establishment on port 23
NVT handling at both ends
Data flow: Client → NVT → Server and vice versa
Security issues: Plain text transmission, no encryption, weak auth
Alternatives: SSH (Secure Shell) on port 22
SSH features: encryption, public key authentication, compression
Modern use: mainly for testing/debugging network services

questions.question: Write and explain the Telnet commands used for session management with their purposes.
keyPoints: open: Establish connection to remote host
close: Close current Telnet connection
quit: Exit Telnet client
status: Display current connection status
mode: Set mode (character/line)
set: Set parameters (echo, carriage-return)
send: Send special characters (interrupt, abort)
?: Display help information
escape: Set escape character
z: Suspend Telnet session
